purpose: Control and measure gas flow rates for multiple gas species in semiconductor processing with high precision

states.name: calibration_drift
states.description: Flow measurement calibration accuracy degradation over time

states.name: valve_wear_level
states.description: Valve component wear degradation from actuation cycles

states.name: zero_stability_drift
states.description: Zero flow reading stability degradation over time

states.name: actual_flow_tracking
states.description: Actual flow rate tracking the setpoint with controller dynamics

events.name: over_temperature
events.description: Internal temperature exceeds maximum safe operating limit

events.name: valve_drive_alarm
events.description: Valve drive level exceeds normal operating range

events.name: controller_error
events.description: Persistent error between setpoint and actual flow

events.name: memory_failure
events.description: EEPROM checksum error indicating memory corruption

parameters.name: calibration_drift_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for calibration drift degradation

parameters.name: valve_wear_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for valve wear degradation

parameters.name: zero_drift_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for zero stability drift

parameters.name: internal_temperature_variation
parameters.description: Temperature variation factor affecting zero drift

parameters.name: flow_tracking_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for flow tracking

state_transition_logic: calibration_drift decreases by (1/calibration_drift_timescale) * (flow_setpoint/100) * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
valve_wear_level decreases by (1/valve_wear_timescale) * (valve_drive_level/100) * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
zero_stability_drift decreases by (1/zero_drift_timescale) * internal_temperature_variation * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
flow_totalized_state increases by thermal_mass_flow_rate * dt / 60000, clamped to [0.0, inf]
actual_flow_tracking approaches flow_setpoint using first-order lag with coefficient min(flow_tracking_coefficient, 0.5), clamped to [0.0, inf]
event_logic: high_limit_alarm triggers when actual_flow_tracking > flow_setpoint * high_flow_threshold / 100
low_limit_alarm triggers when actual_flow_tracking < flow_setpoint * low_flow_threshold / 100
system_error triggers when calibration_drift < controller_error_threshold OR valve_wear_level < controller_error_threshold
valve_drive_alarm triggers when valve_wear_level < 0.2 (requiring excessive drive)
controller_error triggers when calibration_drift < controller_error_threshold AND valve_wear_level < controller_error_threshold
assumptions: Calibration drift is primarily driven by flow usage and thermal cycling
Valve wear increases with actuation frequency and drive level
Zero drift is influenced by temperature variations and sensor aging
Flow tracking dynamics follow first-order response to setpoint changes
Multiple gas species share common degradation mechanisms but have independent flow paths
Temperature rise indicates valve wear due to increased friction and power dissipation
Flow totalization is purely accumulative with no reset mechanism modeled
